value.strategy: At just over 40% in equities, we remain defensive. Where we have added in the past year, it is to stocks that either sit outside the current AI narrative or those whose business models, we believe, will succeed regardless of the endgame. We retain just under a third of the portfolio in index-linked bonds, both UK and US, having added to the former this year. Duration is deliberately short at just under two years, reflective of the risk that fiscal and, for the US, geopolitical risks come home to roost. This, combined with the portfolio’s c. 20% in liquidity puts us in a strong position to take advantage of volatility when it returns. We reduced our net dollar exposure from 25% to 8% in the weeks following Liberation Day on April 2nd. We reduced US TIPS in favour of UK Linkers in 2025 and reduced duration materially. By the end of the year, we had also built a 10% holding in short-dated Japanese government bonds.
value.philosophy: Our aim is to protect investors’ capital and increase its value over the long term. Valuation continues to guide every decision. We continue to believe that much caution is warranted but we remain open minded given how little is known at this stage. It is our belief however that an understanding of markets and the macroeconomic outlook is vastly improved by meeting with businesses at the heart of change.
value.selection_process: We took advantage of lower valuations in stocks we favour to increase our equity allocation by around 10 percentage points. Where we have added in the past year, it is to stocks that either sit outside the current AI narrative or those whose business models, we believe, will succeed regardless of the endgame. Valuation continues to guide every decision.
value.asset_types: Our equities performed respectably. We have maintained our holding in gold at circa 12% throughout the year. We reduced US TIPS in favour of UK Linkers in 2025. By the end of the year, we had also built a 10% holding in short-dated Japanese government bonds. We retain just under a third of the portfolio in index-linked bonds, both UK and US. The portfolio’s c. 20% in liquidity.
value.real_objectives: Our aim is to protect investors’ capital and increase its value over the long term. At just over 40% in equities, we remain defensive. This, combined with the portfolio’s c. 20% in liquidity puts us in a strong position to take advantage of volatility when it returns.
value.fund_history: The strategy delivered returns of around 9-10% for the 2025 calendar year. The strategy fell just over -2% over the same time frame [February to April 2025]. We took advantage of lower valuations in stocks we favour to increase our equity allocation by around 10 percentage points. We did half of this on Monday 7th April. We reduced our net dollar exposure from 25% to 8% in the weeks following Liberation Day on April 2nd. We reduced US TIPS in favour of UK Linkers in 2025 and reduced duration materially. By the end of the year, we had also built a 10% holding in short-dated Japanese government bonds. Where we have added in the past year, it is to stocks that either sit outside the current AI narrative or those whose business models, we believe, will succeed regardless of the endgame. We retain just under a third of the portfolio in index-linked bonds, both UK and US, having added to the former this year.

extracted_from: Our aim is to protect investors’ capital and increase its value over the long term.
